query: What do users really need here? They say they want a dashboard but I'm not sure.
Separate the functional job from the emotional job for this workflow.
note: Three-dimension jobs framework is core to this skill.

query: I think there's a latent need we're missing — how do I surface it?
note: Latent need surfacing is explicitly covered.

query: Users keep apologizing for using spreadsheets alongside our product. What does that tell us?
note: Workarounds/apologies are latent-need signals named in the skill.

query: How do I move past what users are asking for to what they actually need?
note: Wants-vs-needs ladder is the primary technique.

query: What's the social job behind choosing a JetBrains IDE over VS Code?
note: Social jobs are explicitly part of the three-dimension framework.

query: Write job statements for our three most common user scenarios.
Help me write a job statement for an engineering manager onboarding a new hire.
Define a persona for our enterprise buyer — demographics, role, company size.
note: NOT-for clause: demographics belong to persona-definition.

query: Map the stakeholders for this rollout on a power-interest matrix.
note: NOT-for clause: stakeholder mapping belongs to persona-mapping.

query: Write an interview guide for our user research study next week.
note: NOT-for clause: interview scripting belongs to elicitation.

query: Help me frame whether we're solving the right problem at all.
note: Near-miss — problem framing belongs to problem-definition, upstream of needs.

query: Write the value proposition for this product.
note: Near-miss — value prop belongs to value-proposition-design.
